description: Invalid pointer in SpiderMonkey module linking. CVE-2026-2785 (MFSA 2026-15, Firefox 148). For cyclic (parsed) modules, Parser::moduleBody injects a hidden *namespace* binding into the module's scope so that re-exporting an `import * as ns` binding resolves to a slot on the module environment. Synthetic modules (JSON, CSS, bytes modules, etc.) reuse the same ModuleEnvironmentObject::createSynthetic path, but their shape — built by js::CreateEnvironmentShapeForSyntheticModule in js/src/vm/Scope.cpp — only enumerated the synthetic export names and did NOT include the *namespace* property. As a result, a re-exporter module like `import * as ns from 'jsonmod' with { type: 'json' }; export { ns };` forced the resolver to look up or write the *namespace* slot on a synthetic env that had no matching slot in its shape, accessing an out-of-bounds / uninitialized fixed-slot index inside the environment's reserved-slot region. The fix (commit 3b5800515463f1a9c676a744f8bd4e1256247020 by Jon Coppeard, 2026-02-06, Phabricator D281434) prepends `cx->names().star_namespace_star_` as the first property in CreateEnvironmentShapeForSyntheticModule, shifts ModuleEnvironmentObject::firstSyntheticValueSlot() from RESERVED_SLOTS to RESERVED_SLOTS+1, updates the propMapLength assertion to `values.length()+1`, and adds MOZ_ASSERT_IF(result, !hasSyntheticModuleFields()) to hasCyclicModuleFields to prevent synthetic modules from accidentally taking the cyclic path. Tests landed separately at commit 270ac1af7c1528a00f713f8b3e92c396bc8938f6 as js/src/jit-test/tests/modules/bug-2013549-{1,2}.js. Reported by Evyatar Ben Asher, Keane Lucas, Nicholas Carlini, Newton Cheng, Daniel Freeman, Alex Gaynor, and Joel Weinberger using Claude from Anthropic.
